segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Good morning.
segments.zh: 早上好，乔，感谢您来我们中介这边见面。我是中介代表之一，我叫莎莉·贝克。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Thanks for coming in to see us here at the agency, Joe.

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: I'm one of the agency representatives, and my name's Sally Baker.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Hi Sally.
segments.zh: 嗨，莎莉。我想我们通过电话，对吧？

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: I think we spoke on the phone, didn't we?

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: That's right, we did.
segments.zh: 没错，是的。所以感谢您寄来简历。我们仔细看过了，觉得有两份工作可能适合您。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: So thank you for sending in your CV.

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: We've had quite a careful look at it and I think we have two jobs that might be suitable for you.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: The first one is in a company based in North London.
segments.zh: 第一份是在一家位于伦敦北部的公司。他们正在招聘一名行政助理。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: They're looking for an administrative assistant.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。是什么样的公司？

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: What sort of company is it?

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: They're called Home Solutions and they design and make furniture.
segments.zh: 公司叫 Home Solutions，他们设计和制造家具。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Oh, I don't know much about that, but it sounds interesting.
segments.zh: 哦，我对那个不太了解，但听起来挺有趣的。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Yes, well as I said, they want someone in their office, and looking at your past experience it does look as if you fit quite a few of the requirements.
segments.zh: 是的，正如我所说，他们办公室需要人，而且从你的过往经历来看，你似乎符合不少要求。你的简历上显示你做过一些数据录入工作？

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: So on your CV it appears you've done some data entry?

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: So that's one skill they want.
segments.zh: 这是他们需要的一项技能。另外，他们希望录用的人能参加会议并做记录……

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Then they expect the person they appoint to attend meetings and take notes there ...

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的，我以前做过这个。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: I've done that before, yes.

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: And you'd need to be able to cope with general admin.
segments.zh: 而且你需要能够处理一般的行政工作，比如归档、保存记录等等。那应该没问题。在我上一份工作中，我还需要管理日程表。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Filing, and keeping records and so on?

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: That should be OK.

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: And in my last job I also had to manage the diary.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Excellent.
segments.zh: 太好了。这也是他们这里需要的。我建议你把它加到简历里——我记得你没提过这个，对吧？

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: That's something they want here too.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: I'd suggest you add it to your CV – I don't think you mentioned that, did you?

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: No.
segments.zh: 没有。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: So as far as the requirements go, they want good computer skills, of course, and they particularly mention spreadsheets.
segments.zh: 至于要求方面，他们当然需要良好的计算机技能，并且特别提到了电子表格。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: That should be fine.
segments.zh: 那应该没问题。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: And interpersonal skills – which would be something they'd check with your references.
segments.zh: 还有人际交往能力——他们会通过你的推荐人来核实这一点。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: I think that should be OK, yes.
segments.zh: 我觉得那应该没问题，是的。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Then they mention that they want someone who is careful and takes care with details – just looking at your CV, I'd say you're probably alright there.
segments.zh: 然后他们提到想要一个细心、注重细节的人——单看你的简历，我觉得你在这方面应该没问题。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: I think so, yes.
segments.zh: 我也这么觉得。他们需要什么特殊经验吗？

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Do they want any special experience?

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: I think they wanted some experience of teleconferencing.
segments.zh: 我觉得他们想要一些电话会议的经验。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: I've got three years' experience of that.
segments.zh: 我有三年的相关经验。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Let's see, yes, good.
segments.zh: 让我看看，是的，不错。实际上他们只要求至少一年，所以这很好。那这件事你感兴趣吗？

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: In fact they're only asking for at least one year, so that's great.

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: So is that something that might interest you?

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: It is, yes.
segments.zh: 是的，没错。不过问题是，你说他们在北伦敦，那对我来说通勤距离就太远了。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: The only thing is, you said they were in North London so it would be quite a long commute for me.

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: So as far as the responsibilities for this position go, they want someone who can manage the stock, obviously, and also deliveries.
segments.zh: 那么关于这个职位的职责，他们希望有人能管理库存，当然还有送货。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: That should be OK.
segments.zh: 那应该没问题。你得跟踪记录这些东西，但我对数字一直挺在行的。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: You've got to keep track of stuff, but I've always been quite good with numbers.

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Good. that's their first requirement.
segments.zh: 很好，这是他们的第一个要求。他们还希望有人懂电脑，我们知道你懂。

words.def: 懂电脑的，会使用电脑的

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: And they want someone who's computer literate, which we know you are.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Sure.
segments.zh: 当然。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Then they mention organisational skills.
segments.zh: 然后他们提到了组织能力。他们希望有人条理清晰。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: They want someone who's well organised.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Yes, I think I am.
segments.zh: 是的，我觉得我是。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: And tidy?
segments.zh: 那整洁呢？

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Yes, they go together really, don't they?
segments.zh: 对，这两点其实是一回事，对吧？

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Sure.
segments.zh: 没错。然后就是常规要求了：他们希望应聘者口头和书面沟通能力都强。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Then the usual stuff; they want someone who can communicate well both orally and in writing.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。说到上一份仓库工作，我最喜欢的一点就是团队合作。我觉得那对这份工作真的很重要。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: And for the last warehouse job I had, one of the things I enjoyed most was being part of a team.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: I found that was really essential for the job.

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Excellent.
segments.zh: 很好。是的，他们确实提到希望应聘者习惯团队合作。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Yes, they do mention that they want someone who's used to that, yes.

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Now when you were working in a warehouse last time, what sorts of items were you dealing with?
segments.zh: 那你上次在仓库工作时，主要处理哪些物品？

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: It was mostly bathroom and kitchen equipment, sinks and stoves and fridges.
segments.zh: 主要是卫浴和厨房设备，比如水槽、炉灶和冰箱。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: So you're OK moving heavy things?
segments.zh: 那你搬重东西没问题吧？

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Sure.
segments.zh: 当然。我挺强壮的，而且受过训练。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: I'm quite strong, and I've had the training.

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Good.
segments.zh: 好。那说到经验，他们提到要有驾照，还要有在伦敦开车的经验——这样你才能应付交通什么的。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: Now as far as experience goes, they mention they want someone with a licence, and that you have experience of driving in London – so you can cope with the traffic and so on.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Yes, no problem.
segments.zh: 是的，没问题。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: And you've got experience of warehouse work ... and the final thing they mention is customer service.
segments.zh: 你还有仓库工作的经验……最后他们还提到了客户服务。我看你的简历，这方面应该没问题。

segments.speaker: SALLY
segments.en: I think looking at your CV you're OK there.

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。那工资呢？能再跟我说说吗？

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: So what about pay?

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Can you tell me a bit more about that, please ...

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: My guest on the show today is Alice Riches who started the Street Play Scheme where she lives in Beechwood Road.
segments.zh: 今天节目的嘉宾是爱丽丝·里奇斯，她在她住的比奇伍德路发起了“街头游戏计划”。可能有些人还不知道——街头游戏就是让当地居民把街道封闭几个小时，这样孩子们就能安全地在街上玩耍。她先是在自己住的比奇伍德路搞起来的，这个想法后来流行开来，现在全市都有街头游戏计划了。那么爱丽丝，你是什么时候开始这个计划的？

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: For those of you that don't already know – Street Play involves local residents closing off their street for a few hours so that children have a chance to play in the street safely.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: She started it in her own street, Beechwood Road, and the idea caught on, and there are now Street Play Schemes all over the city.

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: So when did you actually start the scheme, Alice?

segments.speaker: PRESENTER
segments.en: So what actually happens when Beechwood Road is closed?
segments.zh: 那比奇伍德路封闭的时候，具体会发生什么呢？

items.prompt: Administrative assistant in a company that produces 1 ________ (North London)

pairs.note: produce 和 make 是同义词，都表示'生产'。
题干说'生产家具的公司'，原文说'设计和制造家具'，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到公司名称 Home Solutions，并说明他们设计和制造家具，因此答案应为 furniture。

pairs.note: go to 和 attend 都表示'参加'，是同义替换。
原文和题干完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'go to'在原文中被替换为'attend'，而'take notes'完全一致，因此定位到'attend meetings and take notes'，答案就是'meetings'。

pairs.note: 题干是名词 management，原文是动词 manage，词性变化但意思相同。
题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问 management of 3 ________，原文中 'manage the diary' 直接对应，注意 manage 是动词形式，但意思与 management 相同。

pairs.note: attention to 和 takes care with 都表示'关注、注意'，是同义替换。
答案直接来自原文中的 details。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'关注什么'，原文说'takes care with details'，所以答案就是 details。

items.prompt: need a minimum of 5 ________ of experience of teleconferencing

pairs.note: minimum 和 at least 都表示'至少'，是常见同义替换。
题干说5年，原文说1年，数字不同，注意否定替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'至少5年经验'，但原文说'至少1年'，所以答案不是5，而是1年。通过'minimum'和'at least'的替换定位。

pairs.note: 题干中的managing是动名词，原文用动词原形manage，意思相同。
原文直接出现deliveries，与答案一致。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'manage the stock, obviously, and also deliveries'，其中deliveries就是答案。

pairs.note: organised 和 tidy 都表示有条理、整洁，是同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中 very organised 对应原文中的 tidy，两者意思相近，直接定位到答案。

pairs.note: 题干说'习惯在...工作'，原文说'成为...的一部分'，意思相近，但表达方式不同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'being part of a team'，与题干'used to working in a team'意思一致，因此答案应为'team'。

items.prompt: able to cope with items that are 9 ________

pairs.note: cope with 被替换为 OK moving，表示能处理
items 替换为 things，heavy 相同

paraphrase.explain: 题干问能处理什么物品，定位句说 moving heavy things，所以答案 heavy。

pairs.note: 原文直接出现 service，与题干相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的 service 在原文中直接出现，且 customer 紧邻其前，因此定位到 customer service。
